figures.caption: Table 1: Cloud token savings (%) per tactic in isolation. Local model: Llama 3.2 3B. Cloud model: Gemma 3 4B (simulated locally). Negative values indicate the tactic increased cloud tokens. Mean of two runs (10 samples per workload each); T1–T4 varied by ±2–7 pp; T5–T7 showed higher variance (±3–14 pp) due to model non-determinism and small sample size.

Table 4: Full evaluation metrics per workload and tactic subset (representative run; Tables 1–2 report means of two runs). Cloud tokens = input + output sent to the cloud model. Cost uses OpenAI gpt-4o-mini rate card as a proxy.
Figure 2: Cloud token savings (%) per tactic in isolation. Error bars show the half-range across two runs.
Figure 3: Cloud token savings (%) for tactic combinations. Error bars show the half-range across two runs.
Figure 4: Token savings vs. dollar cost per 10-sample workload. Points closer to the lower-right are Pareto-optimal (more savings, less cost). Faded markers at 0% savings show the baseline.
Figure 1: Pipeline stage ordering. Each stage either answers the request, transforms it, or passes it through. No stage makes a parallel cloud call.
